title: CNN's 'Kara Swisher Wants to Live Forever' Premieres: Longevity Science Goes Primetime
description: CNN's six-part longevity docuseries premiered April 11 at 9pm ET, following journalist Kara Swisher through biotech labs, hyperbaric chambers, ketamine therapy, South Korea's healthcare system, and interviews with Sam Altman, Bryan Johnson, and Jennifer Doudna. The show brings mainstream attention to the longevity industry while distinguishing real science from hype -- a watershed cultural moment for the field.

title: Meal Timing Emerges as Powerful Modulator of Organ-Specific Biological Aging
description: A Lifespan.io-featured study published in npj Science of Food shows that later first and last meals and extended feeding windows are associated with accelerated biological aging across body, heart, and liver clocks. The optimal last-meal window appears to be 3-7pm, with effects varying by organ, sex, diet quality, and caloric intake -- practical chrono-nutrition guidance for longevity enthusiasts.

title: King Penguin Study in Nature Communications: Sedentary, Well-Fed Lifestyle Accelerates Epigenetic Aging
description: Published in Nature Communications, a study comparing wild and zoo-housed king penguins finds that a sedentary, well-fed lifestyle -- analogous to the Western human lifestyle -- accelerates epigenetic aging by 2.5 to 6.5 years (comparable to smoking effects in humans). Crucially, zoo penguins still lived longer overall, suggesting Western lifestyles extend lifespan but not healthspan.

title: BioAge Labs Highlights 50-Year Longitudinal Human Aging Cohort Platform for Drug Discovery
description: BioAge Labs posted on X (April 9) detailing how their discovery platform draws on longitudinal human aging cohorts spanning 50+ years to identify the most impactful biology of aging. The approach represents a data-first paradigm shift in longevity drug development -- starting from human aging data rather than working backward from animal models.

title: A4M Longevity SpringFest 2026 Wraps: Exosomes, GLP-1 Mastery, and Regenerative Medicine Dominate
description: The A4M Longevity SpringFest 2026 wrapped its four-day run in West Palm Beach (April 9-12), featuring a full-day Exosomes Masterclass, GLP-1/mTOR mastery sessions, and Dr. Michael Roizen's keynote on reversing biological age. The event signals clinical translation of longevity science is accelerating, with exosomes and regenerative medicine emerging as the dominant themes for 2026 practice.

title: CNN: What Tech Bros Are Getting Wrong About Longevity -- and What You Can Get Right
description: Companion piece to the docuseries premiere, CNN examines how wealth-driven longevity pursuits differ from evidence-based health extension. Swisher emphasizes we live in a 'sick care' not health care society, and that sleep, diet, exercise, and community remain the most impactful interventions.

title: Kara Swisher Rates Longevity Trends in CNN Digital Special
description: In a digital-exclusive segment, Swisher evaluates which longevity trends deserve attention and which are hype, providing her 'kill, marry' ranking of popular interventions ahead of the series premiere.

title: Meal Timing Impacts Biological Aging Across Multiple Organs
description: Study finds eating the last meal between 3-5pm slows whole-body and heart aging versus after 9pm. Extended feeding windows over 16 hours are associated with faster aging, with effects varying by organ, sex, diet quality, and caloric intake.

title: NMN Plus Apigenin Restores Muscle, Bone, and Gut Health in Aged Mice via SIRT3
description: Combining NMN (NAD+ precursor) with apigenin (CD38 inhibitor) restores NAD+ levels, reduces senescence markers, and improves muscle strength, bone density, and gut microbiome diversity in aged mice. Benefits were partially SIRT3-dependent.

title: Somatostatin Converts Brain Microglia to Protective Mode, Clears Amyloid in Alzheimer's Mice
source: Medical Xpress / Brain, Behavior, and Immunity
description: DGIST researchers showed somatostatin directly regulates microglia to suppress neuroinflammation, reduce amyloid beta accumulation, and improve spatial memory in Alzheimer's model mice, opening drug repurposing pathways.

title: King Penguin Zoo Study: Sedentary Lifestyle Accelerates Epigenetic Aging by 2.5-6.5 Years
description: Zoo-housed king penguins show accelerated epigenetic aging comparable to smoking effects in humans, yet still live longer. Findings suggest Western lifestyles extend lifespan but compress healthspan.

title: Nature Feature: Partial Cellular Reprogramming About to Be Tested in Humans
description: Nature published an in-depth feature on Life Biosciences' ER-100, the first partial epigenetic reprogramming therapy entering human trials for glaucoma and NAION using three Yamanaka factors.

title: Life Biosciences' FDA-Cleared Trial: Is the FDA Warming to Rejuvenation?
description: Lifespan.io analyzes how Life Biosciences' ER-100 IND clearance signals the FDA is establishing a measured playbook for longevity developers, focused on specific diseases with accepted clinical outcomes rather than treating aging itself.

title: Targeting Longevity 2026 Congress: Aging Reframed as Loss of Biological Coordination
description: The 2nd World Congress on Targeting Longevity in Berlin (April 8-9) brought together researchers in mitochondria, microbiota, and senescence to reframe aging as progressive loss of coordination between biological systems.

title: Novel RNA Therapy Encourages Heart Self-Repair via Atrial Natriuretic Peptide
description: RNA-lipid nanoparticles encoding Nppa in skeletal muscle produce pro-ANP converted to ANP by Corin in the heart, reducing scarring and improving cardiac function in animal models with a single injection.

title: High-Dose Influenza Vaccine Linked to Lower Alzheimer's Risk in Adults Over 65
description: Retrospective cohort study of 120,775 adults aged 65+ found high-dose influenza vaccination associated with lower Alzheimer's dementia risk in the first 25 months compared to standard-dose.

title: AhR Inhibition Promotes Axon Regeneration After Spinal Cord Injury (Published in Nature)
description: Published in Nature, aryl hydrocarbon receptor inhibition promotes axon regrowth in peripheral and spinal cord injury models via HIF1a, shifting cells from stress signaling to growth signaling.

title: BioAge Labs Highlights 50-Year Human Aging Cohort Discovery Platform
description: BioAge Labs describes how their platform draws on longitudinal human aging cohorts spanning 50+ years to identify the most impactful biology of aging for drug development.

title: Rubedo Life Sciences Phase 1 RLS-1496: First GPX4 Modulator Shows Senolytic Efficacy in Humans
description: Rubedo's first-in-class GPX4 modulator RLS-1496 met its primary endpoint in Phase 1, with dose-dependent senescent cell reduction and improvement in psoriasis, atopic dermatitis, and skin aging.

title: UT Health San Antonio Launches Multi-Phase Rapamycin, Semaglutide, and Dapagliflozin Trial
description: NIA-funded study will test rapamycin, semaglutide, and dapagliflozin in over 700 older adults to determine optimal dosing and whether these drugs slow biological aging.

title: Enlivex Wins FDA IND Clearance for Allocetra Phase 2b in Age-Related Knee Osteoarthritis
description: Enlivex received FDA clearance for a Phase 2b trial of Allocetra immunotherapy in age-related knee osteoarthritis, following positive Phase 1/2a data in 134 patients showing durable treatment effects in older populations.

title: Longevity Biotech Investment Q1 2026: $3.74B Raised Across 49 Deals
description: Q1 2026 saw 49 financing events raising $3.74 billion, a 56% increase over Q1 2025, with a projected full-year range of $8-9 billion in longevity biotech investment.

title: Eli Lilly Signs $2.75B Deal with Insilico Medicine for AI-Developed Oral Therapeutics
description: Eli Lilly expanded its AI drug development partnership with Insilico Medicine in a deal worth up to $2.75 billion for exclusive worldwide licensing of preclinical oral therapeutics.

title: Longevity Funding Tracker: 12 Pure-Play Deals Raise $530M+ Since Late 2025
description: Twelve longevity pure-players disclosed new funding led by Function Health's $298M Series B at $2.5B valuation, with deals spanning reprogramming, mTOR inhibitors, and diagnostics.

title: The Senolytics Pipeline in 2026: Three Lanes of Development
description: Comprehensive pipeline analysis: senolytics splitting into academic D+Q combinations, localized precision therapies (DME, dermatology), and next-gen discovery including GPX4 modulation and immune-mediated clearance.

title: David Sinclair: Longevity Research Being 'Starved of Funds' -- Assembling Team for April Launch
description: David Sinclair posted that longevity research is being starved of funds when the US needs to be more competitive, announcing he has assembled a team with a launch date of April 2026.

title: Bryan Johnson's Blueprint Protocol 2026 Update: Refined Diet, Rapamycin Dropped
description: Blueprint 2026 refines macros to 2,250 cal with 130g protein, eliminates rapamycin due to side effects, and emphasizes measurement-first supplement decisions based on lab results.

title: Alex Zhavoronkov: Longevity Biotech Crossing from 'Interesting Thesis' to Mainstream
description: Insilico Medicine's Alex Zhavoronkov declares 2026 the breakout year for longevity biotech, with real translational science, clearer clinical pathways, and investors treating aging biology as serious risk-return.

title: London Longevity Event April 16: Addressing the Translation Gap in Aging Drug Development
description: London Longevity convenes founders, researchers, and pharma BD leaders on April 16 to confront the bottlenecks between aging biology discovery and clinical-stage therapeutics, co-organized with age1.

title: New BMJ Open Study: All US States Show Universal Longevity Gains from 1941-2000
description: University of Wisconsin-Madison researchers challenge earlier estimates, finding universal cohort life expectancy gains of 7-16 years across all US states, with less geographic disparity than previously believed.
